description:
Model a two-story 'cargotecture' building using two intersecting 40ft High Cube shipping containers, and install solar panels on the available roof areas.

Instructions:
1. Ensure SketchUp Make 2017 is open with a new, empty model and units set to Meters.
2. Model Container A (Lower): Draw a 12.0m (Length) × 2.4m (Width) rectangle on the ground plane (aligned with the Red/East-West axis). Use Push/Pull to extrude it to a height of 2.9m. Group this geometry.
3. Model Container B (Upper): Create an identical 12.0m × 2.4m × 2.9m container. Rotate it 90 degrees so it aligns with the Green/North-South axis.
4. Move Container B so it rests exactly on top of the center of Container A. The bottom of Container B should be at Z=2.9m. From above, the building should look like a symmetric cross or plus (+) sign.
5. Use the Skelion plugin to install solar panels on the roof of Container B (the top-most face). Configure them as Landscape orientation and flush mount (0 tilt).
6. Use Skelion to install solar panels on the exposed roof 'wings' of Container A (the left and right sections not covered by Container B). 
7. CRITICAL: Ensure no panels are placed inside the intersection or underneath Container B. You may need to draw lines to separate the faces or delete colliding panels.
8. Save the completed model to: C:\Users\Docker\Documents\container_project.skp
